feature: Three New Features: Content Management, Direct Messaging, Analytics Dashboard
summary: Tested all three new features. Backend: 22/25 tests passed (3 skipped due to route conflict). Frontend: All 3 pages load correctly with proper components. Core functionality works for all features.

critical.issue: Route conflict - media_routes.py catches /content/{id} before content_routes.py
critical.details: media_routes.py has routes like /content/{media_id} without prefix, registered before content_routes.py which has prefix /content. This causes GET/PUT/DELETE by ID to hit media_routes instead of content_routes.
critical.solution: Either rename media_routes content endpoints or reorder router registration

frontend_tests.details: Login works, authenticated navbar shows Messages, Analytics, Content links
Shows page title, Upload Content button, content grid with 5 items, search/filter controls, visibility badges, stats
Shows conversation list, chat interface, message bubbles, input field, send button, New Conversation button
4 tabs (Overview, Content, Audience, Revenue), stat cards, charts, content performance table
Messages, Analytics visible in navbar, My Content under Content dropdown

blocked_operations: Delete - due to route conflict with media_routes

action_items: FIX: Route conflict between media_routes.py and content_routes.py - media_routes catches /content/{id} before content_routes
Option 2: Register content_router before media_router in server.py
Option 3: Use unique path prefix for content_routes like /user-content
critical_code_review_comments: Route conflict: media_routes.py (line 214-332) has /content/{media_id} without prefix, registered before content_routes.py which uses /content prefix. Both resolve to /api/content/{id} - FastAPI matches first route.
content_routes uses user_content collection, media_routes uses media_content collection - different data stores but same API path

success_rate.backend: 88% (22/25 passed, 3 skipped due to route conflict)

seed_data_creation: Test content items created during pytest (TEST_PyTest_Track, etc), test messages sent
context_for_next_testing_agent: Three new features tested. Content Management has a route conflict issue where /api/content/{id} endpoints are caught by media_routes.py instead of content_routes.py - upload and list work but GET/PUT/DELETE by ID fail. Direct Messaging and Analytics Dashboard fully functional. All frontend pages render correctly with proper data-testid attributes.

rca_of_the_issue.issue: Content Management GET/PUT/DELETE by ID returns 404 'Media not found'
rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: Route registration order in server.py - media_router (line 253) registered before content_router (line 261). media_routes.py has /content/{media_id} without prefix, content_routes.py uses prefix='/content'. Both resolve to /api/content/{id}, FastAPI matches first registered route (media_routes).
reproduction_steps: 1. Upload content via POST /api/content/upload - works (matches exact path)
2. List content via GET /api/content - works (matches exact path)
3. Get content by ID via GET /api/content/{id} - fails (matches media_routes /content/{media_id} first)
rca_of_the_issue.mitigation: Either change media_routes /content/* paths to /media/* or reorder router registration in server.py
